1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: class MergeRequest < ApplicationRecord
4:   extend Gitlab::Cache::RequestCache
5: 
6:   include AtomicInternalId
7:   include IidRoutes
8:   include Issuable
9:   include Noteable
10:   include Referable
11:   include Presentable
12:   include TimeTrackable
13:   include ManualInverseAssociation
14:   include EachBatch
15:   include ThrottledTouch
16:   include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
17:   include LabelEventable
18:   include ReactiveCaching
19:   include FromUnion
20:   include DeprecatedAssignee
21:   include ShaAttribute
22:   include MilestoneEventable
23:   include StateEventable
24:   include Approvable
25:   include IdInOrdered
26:   include Todoable
27:   include Spammable
28: 
29:   extend ::Gitlab::Utils::Override
30: 
31:   sha_attribute :squash_commit_sha
32:   sha_attribute :merge_ref_sha
33: 
34:   self.reactive_cache_key = ->(model) { [model.project.id, model.iid] }
35:   self.reactive_cache_refresh_interval = 10.minutes
36:   self.reactive_cache_lifetime = 10.minutes
37:   self.reactive_cache_work_type = :no_dependency
38: 
39:   SORTING_PREFERENCE_FIELD = :merge_requests_sort
40:   CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DESCRIPTION_MAX_LENGTH = 2700
41:   MERGE_LEASE_TIMEOUT = 15.minutes.to_i
42: 
43:   belongs_to :target_project, class_name: "Project"
44:   belongs_to :source_project, class_name: "Project"
45:   belongs_to :merge_user, class_name: "User"
46: 
47:   has_internal_id :iid, scope: :target_project, track_if: -> { !importing? },
48:     init: ->(mr, scope) do
49:       if mr
50:         mr.target_project&.merge_requests&.maximum(:iid)
51:       elsif scope[:project]
52:         where(target_project: scope[:project]).maximum(:iid)
53:       end
54:     end
55: 
56:   has_many :merge_request_diffs,
57:     -> { regular }, inverse_of: :merge_request
58:   has_many :merge_request_context_commits, inverse_of: :merge_request
59:   has_many :merge_request_context_commit_diff_files, through: :merge_request_context_commits, source: :diff_files
60: 
61:   has_many :generated_ref_commits,
62:     primary_key: [:iid, :target_project_id],
63:     class_name: 'MergeRequests::GeneratedRefCommit',
64:     foreign_key: [:merge_request_iid, :project_id],
65:     inverse_of: :merge_request
66: 
67:   has_one :merge_request_diff,
68:     -> { regular.order('merge_request_diffs.id DESC') }, inverse_of: :merge_request
69:   # Enabling inverse relationship causes previously cached merge_head_diff to be returned as merge_request_diff
70:   # MR: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/merge_requests/214072
71:   has_one :merge_head_diff,
72:     -> { merge_head }, inverse_of: false, class_name: 'MergeRequestDiff'
73:   has_one :cleanup_schedule, inverse_of: :merge_request
74:   has_one :predictions, inverse_of: :merge_request
75:   delegate :suggested_reviewers, to: :predictions
76: 
77:   has_one :merge_schedule, class_name: 'MergeRequests::MergeSchedule', inverse_of: :merge_request
78:   has_one :metrics, inverse_of: :merge_request, autosave: true
79:   # NOTE: We do not want to autosave merge_data as this is saved manually
80:   has_one :merge_data, class_name: 'MergeRequests::MergeData', inverse_of: :merge_request, foreign_key: :merge_request_id, autosave: false
81: 
82:   belongs_to :latest_merge_request_diff, class_name: 'MergeRequestDiff'
83:   manual_inverse_association :latest_merge_request_diff, :merge_request
84: 
85:   # method overridden in EE
86:   def suggested_reviewer_users
87:     User.none
88:   end
89: 
90:   # This is the same as latest_merge_request_diff unless:
91:   # 1. There are arguments - in which case we might be trying to force-reload.
92:   # 2. This association is already loaded.
93:   # 3. The latest diff does not exist.
94:   # 4. It doesn't have any merge_request_diffs - it returns an empty MergeRequestDiff
95:   #
96:   # The second one in particular is important - MergeRequestDiff#merge_request
97:   # is the inverse of MergeRequest#merge_request_diff, which means it may not be
98:   # the latest diff, because we could have loaded any diff from this particular
99:   # MR. If we haven't already loaded a diff, then it's fine to load the latest.
100:   def merge_request_diff
101:     fallback = latest_merge_request_diff unless association(:merge_request_diff).loaded?
102: 
103:     fallback || super || MergeRequestDiff.new(merge_request_id: id)
104:   end
105: 
106:   belongs_to :head_pipeline, class_name: "Ci::Pipeline", inverse_of: :merge_requests_as_head_pipeline
107: 
108:   has_many :events, as: :target, dependent: :delete_all # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordDependent
109: 
110:   has_many :merge_request_issues,
111:     class_name: 'MergeRequestsClosingIssues',
112:     inverse_of: :merge_request,
113:     dependent: :delete_all # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordDependent
114: 
115:   has_many :merge_request_closing_issues,
116:     -> { link_type_closes },
117:     class_name: 'MergeRequestsClosingIssues',
118:     inverse_of: :merge_request
119: 
120:   has_one :approval_metrics,
121:     class_name: 'MergeRequest::ApprovalMetrics',
122:     inverse_of: :merge_request
123: 
124:   has_many :cached_closes_issues,
125:     through: :merge_request_closing_issues,
126:     source: :issue
127:   has_many :pipelines_for_merge_request, foreign_key: 'merge_request_id', class_name: 'Ci::Pipeline', inverse_of: :merge_request
128:   has_many :suggestions, through: :notes
129:   has_many :unresolved_notes, -> { unresolved }, as: :noteable, class_name: 'Note', inverse_of: :noteable
130: 
131:   has_many :merge_request_assignees
132:   has_many :assignees, class_name: "User", through: :merge_request_assignees
133:   has_many :merge_request_reviewers
134:   has_many :reviewers, class_name: "User", through: :merge_request_reviewers
135:   has_many :user_mentions, class_name: "MergeRequestUserMention", dependent: :delete_all # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordDependent
136: 
137:   has_many :deployment_merge_requests
138: 
139:   # These are deployments created after the merge request has been merged, and
140:   # the merge request was tracked explicitly (instead of implicitly using a CI
141:   # build).
142:   has_many :deployments,
143:     through: :deployment_merge_requests
144: 
145:   has_many :draft_notes
146:   has_many :reviews, inverse_of: :merge_request
147:   has_many :reviewed_by_users, -> { distinct }, through: :reviews, source: :author
148:   has_many :created_environments, class_name: 'Environment', foreign_key: :merge_request_id, inverse_of: :merge_request
149:   has_many :assignment_events, class_name: 'ResourceEvents::MergeRequestAssignmentEvent', inverse_of: :merge_request
150: 
151:   KNOWN_MERGE_PARAMS = [
152:     :auto_merge_strategy,
153:     :should_remove_source_branch,
154:     :force_remove_source_branch,
155:     :commit_message,
156:     :squash_commit_message,
157:     :sha,
158:     :skip_ci
159:   ].freeze
160:   serialize :merge_params, type: Hash # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordSerialize
161: 
162:   before_validation :set_draft_status
163: 
164:   after_create :ensure_merge_request_diff, unless: :skip_ensure_merge_request_diff
165:   after_update :clear_memoized_shas
166:   after_update :reload_diff_if_branch_changed
167:   after_save :keep_around_commit, unless: :importing?
168:   after_save :save_merge_data_changes
169:   after_commit :enqueue_keep_around_commit, unless: :importing?
170:   after_commit :ensure_metrics!, on: [:create, :update], unless: :importing?
171:   after_commit :expire_etag_cache, unless: :importing?
172: 
173:   after_find :preload_branches
174: 
175:   # When this attribute is true some MR validation is ignored
176:   # It allows us to close or modify broken merge requests
177:   attr_accessor :allow_broken, :skip_branch_existence_check
178: 
179:   # Temporary flag to skip merge_request_diff creation on create.
180:   # See https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/merge_requests/100390
181:   attr_accessor :skip_ensure_merge_request_diff
182: 
183:   # Temporary fields to store compare vars
184:   # when creating new merge request
185:   attr_accessor :can_be_created, :compare_commits, :diff_options, :compare
186: 
187:   # Flag to skip triggering mergeRequestMergeStatusUpdated GraphQL subscription.
188:   attr_accessor :skip_merge_status_trigger
189: 
190:   attr_spammable :title, spam_title: true
191:   attr_spammable :description, spam_description: true
192: 
193:   participant :reviewers
194: 
195:   # Keep states definition to be evaluated before the state_machine block to
196:   #   avoid spec failures. If this gets evaluated after, the `merged` and `locked`
197:   #   states (which are overridden) can be nil.
198:   #
199:   def self.available_state_names
200:     super + [:merged, :locked]
201:   end
202: 
203:   state_machine :state_id, initial: :opened, initialize: false do
204:     event :close do
205:       transition [:opened] => :closed
206:     end
207: 
208:     event :mark_as_merged do
209:       transition [:opened, :locked] => :merged
210:     end
211: 
212:     event :reopen do
213:       transition closed: :opened
214:     end
215: 
216:     event :lock_mr do
217:       transition [:opened] => :locked
218:     end
219: 
220:     event :unlock_mr do
221:       transition locked: :opened
222:     end
223: 
224:     before_transition any => [:opened, :merged] do |merge_request|
225:       merge_request.merge_jid = nil
226:     end
227: 
228:     before_transition any => :closed do |merge_request|
229:       merge_request.merge_error = nil
230:     end
231: 
232:     before_transition any => :merged do |merge_request|
233:       merge_request.merge_error = nil
234:       merge_request.metrics.first_contribution = true if merge_request.first_contribution?
235:     end
236: 
237:     after_transition any => :opened do |merge_request|
238:       merge_request.run_after_commit do
239:         UpdateHeadPipelineForMergeRequestWorker.perform_async(merge_request.id)
240:       end
241:     end
242: 
243:     state :opened, value: MergeRequest.available_states[:opened]
244:     state :closed, value: MergeRequest.available_states[:closed]
245:     state :merged, value: MergeRequest.available_states[:merged]
246:     state :locked, value: MergeRequest.available_states[:locked]
247:   end
248: 
249:   # NOTE: If you modify this state machine (transitions or callbacks), you must also
250:   # review and update the batch method below (batch_mark_as_unchecked).
251:   # Batch methods bypass callbacks for performance, so any new callbacks may need
252:   # to be handled manually.
253:   #
254:   # See: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/work_items/546431
255:   state_machine :merge_status, initial: :unchecked do
256:     event :mark_as_preparing do
257:       transition [:unchecked, :can_be_merged] => :preparing
258:     end
259: 
260:     event :mark_as_unchecked do
261:       transition [:preparing, :can_be_merged, :checking] => :unchecked
262:       transition [:cannot_be_merged, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :cannot_be_merged_recheck
263:     end
264: 
265:     event :mark_as_checking do
266:       transition unchecked: :checking
267:       transition cannot_be_merged_recheck: :cannot_be_merged_rechecking
268:     end
269: 
270:     event :mark_as_mergeable do
271:       transition [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :can_be_merged
272:     end
273: 
274:     event :mark_as_unmergeable do
275:       transition [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :cannot_be_merged
276:     end
277: 
278:     state :preparing
279:     state :unchecked
280:     state :cannot_be_merged_recheck
281:     state :checking
282:     state :cannot_be_merged_rechecking
283:     state :can_be_merged
284:     state :cannot_be_merged
285: 
286:     around_transition do |merge_request, transition, block|
287:       Gitlab::Timeless.timeless(merge_request, &block)
288:     end
289: 
290:     after_transition any => [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :can_be_merged, :cannot_be_merged] do |merge_request, transition|
291:       next if merge_request.skip_merge_status_trigger
292: 
293:       merge_request.run_after_commit do
294:         GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
295:       end
296:     end
297: 
298:     after_transition any => :can_be_merged do |merge_request, transition|
299:       merge_request.run_after_commit do
300:         # We find it instead of reloading in order not to clear any other attributes
301:         next unless MergeRequest.where(id: merge_request.id, auto_merge_enabled: true).exists?
302: 
303:         AutoMergeProcessWorker.perform_async({ 'merge_request_id' => merge_request.id })
304:       end
305:     end
306: 
307:     # rubocop: disable CodeReuse/ServiceClass
308:     after_transition [:unchecked, :checking] => :cannot_be_merged do |merge_request, _|
309:       merge_request.run_after_commit do
310:         next unless merge_request.notify_conflict?
311: 
312:         publish_code_conflict_event
313:         NotificationService.new.merge_request_unmergeable(merge_request)
314:         TodoService.new.merge_request_became_unmergeable(merge_request)
315:       end
316:     end
317:     # rubocop: enable CodeReuse/ServiceClass
318: 
319:     def check_state?(merge_status)
320:       [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking].include?(merge_status.to_sym)
321:     end
322: 
323:     # rubocop: disable Style/SymbolProc
324:     before_transition { |merge_request| merge_request.enable_transitioning }
325: 
326:     after_transition { |merge_request| merge_request.disable_transitioning }
327:     # rubocop: enable Style/SymbolProc
328:   end
329: 
330:   # NOTE: Batch transition merge_status to unchecked/cannot_be_merged_recheck.
331:   # This bypasses state machine callbacks for performance but mimics their behavior.
332:   #
333:   # Transitions mirror the `mark_as_unchecked` event defined above.
334:   # Process batch updates in batches as there can be many target merge requests.
335:   def self.batch_mark_as_unchecked(mr_ids, mrs_to_trigger: [], batch_size: 1000)
336:     return if mr_ids.blank?
337: 
338:     mr_ids.each_slice(batch_size) do |batch_ids|
339:       where(id: batch_ids)
340:         .where(merge_status: [:preparing, :can_be_merged, :checking])
341:         .update_all(merge_status: :unchecked)
342: 
343:       where(id: batch_ids)
344:         .where(merge_status: [:cannot_be_merged, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking])
345:         .update_all(merge_status: :cannot_be_merged_recheck)
346:     end
347: 
348:     mrs_to_trigger.each do |mr|
349:       GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(mr.reset)
350:     end
351:   end
352: 
353:   # NOTE: Batch transition merge_status to checking/cannot_be_merged_rechecking.
354:   # This bypasses state machine callbacks for performance but mimics their behavior.
355:   #
356:   # Transitions mirror the `mark_as_checking` event defined above.
357:   def self.batch_mark_as_checking(mr_ids, batch_size: 1000)
358:     return if mr_ids.blank?
359: 
360:     mr_ids.each_slice(batch_size) do |batch_ids|
361:       where(id: batch_ids)
362:         .where(merge_status: :unchecked)
363:         .update_all(merge_status: :checking)
364: 
365:       where(id: batch_ids)
366:         .where(merge_status: :cannot_be_merged_recheck)
367:         .update_all(merge_status: :cannot_be_merged_rechecking)
368:     end
369:   end
370: 
371:   def self.batch_clear_merge_error(mr_ids, batch_size: 1000)
372:     return if mr_ids.blank?
373: 
374:     mr_ids.each_slice(batch_size) do |batch_ids|
375:       where(id: batch_ids)
376:         .where.not(merge_error: nil)
377:         .update_all(merge_error: nil)
378:     end
379:   end

381:   # Returns current merge_status except it returns `cannot_be_merged_rechecking` as `checking`
382:   # to avoid exposing unnecessary internal state
383:   def public_merge_status
384:     cannot_be_merged_rechecking? || preparing? ? 'checking' : merge_status
385:   end

762:   def self.order_by_metric_column(metric, direction)
763:     column_expression = MergeRequest::Metrics.arel_table[metric]
764:     column_expression_with_direction = direction == 'ASC' ? column_expression.asc : column_expression.desc
765: 
766:     Gitlab::Pagination::Keyset::Order.build(
767:       [
768:         Gitlab::Pagination::Keyset::ColumnOrderDefinition.new(
769:           attribute_name: "merge_request_metrics_#{metric}",
770:           column_expression: column_expression,
771:           order_expression: column_expression_with_direction.nulls_last,

879:   # Returns all the merge requests from an ActiveRecord:Relation.
880:   #
881:   # This method uses a UNION as it usually operates on the result of
882:   # ProjectsFinder#execute. PostgreSQL in particular doesn't always like queries
883:   # using multiple sub-queries especially when combined with an OR statement.
884:   # UNIONs on the other hand perform much better in these cases.
885:   #
886:   # relation - An ActiveRecord::Relation that returns a list of Projects.
887:   #
888:   # Returns an ActiveRecord::Relation.
889:   def self.in_projects(relation)
890:     # unscoping unnecessary conditions that'll be applied
891:     # when executing `where("merge_requests.id IN (#{union.to_sql})")`
892:     source = unscoped.where(source_project_id: relation)
893:     target = unscoped.where(target_project_id: relation)
894: 
895:     from_union([source, target])
896:   end

898:   # This is used after project import, to reset the IDs to the correct
899:   # values. It is not intended to be called without having already scoped the
900:   # relation.
901:   #
902:   # Only set `regular` merge request diffs as latest so `merge_head` diff
903:   # won't be considered as `MergeRequest#merge_request_diff`.
904:   def self.set_latest_merge_request_diff_ids!
905:     update = "
906:       latest_merge_request_diff_id = (
907:         SELECT MAX(id)
908:         FROM merge_request_diffs
909:         WHERE merge_requests.id = merge_request_diffs.merge_request_id
910:         AND merge_request_diffs.diff_type = #{MergeRequestDiff.diff_types[:regular]}
911:       )".squish
912: 
913:     self.each_batch do |batch|
914:       batch.update_all(update)
915:     end
916:   end

1075:   # Calls `MergeWorker` to proceed with the merge process and
1076:   # updates `merge_jid` with the MergeWorker#jid.
1077:   # This helps tracking enqueued and ongoing merge jobs.
1078:   def merge_async(user_id, params)
1079:     jid = MergeWorker.with_status.perform_async(id, user_id, params.to_h)
1080:     update_merge_jid(jid)
1081: 
1082:     # merge_ongoing? depends on merge_jid
1083:     # expire etag cache since the attribute is changed without triggering callbacks
1084:     expire_etag_cache
1085:   end
1086: 
1087:   # Set off a rebase asynchronously, atomically updating the `rebase_jid` of
1088:   # the MR so that the status of the operation can be tracked.
1089:   def rebase_async(user_id, skip_ci: false)
1090:     with_rebase_lock do
1091:       raise ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError if !open? || rebase_in_progress?
1092: 
1093:       # Although there is a race between setting rebase_jid here and clearing it
1094:       # in the RebaseWorker, it can't do any harm since we check both that the
1095:       # attribute is set *and* that the sidekiq job is still running. So a JID
1096:       # for a completed RebaseWorker is equivalent to a nil JID.
1097:       jid = Sidekiq::Worker.skipping_transaction_check do
1098:         RebaseWorker.with_status.perform_async(id, user_id, skip_ci)
1099:       end
1100: 
1101:       update_column(:rebase_jid, jid)
1102:     end
1103: 
1104:     # rebase_in_progress? depends on rebase_jid
1105:     # expire etag cache since the attribute is changed without triggering callbacks
1106:     expire_etag_cache
1107:   end

1353:   def existing_mrs_targeting_same_branch
1354:     similar_mrs = target_project
1355:         .merge_requests
1356:         .where(source_branch: source_branch, target_branch: target_branch)
1357:         .where(source_project: source_project)
1358:         .opened

1365:   def validate_branches
1366:     return unless target_project && source_project
1367: 
1368:     if target_project == source_project && target_branch == source_branch
1369:       errors.add :branch_conflict, "You can't use same project/branch for source and target"
1370:       return
1371:     end
1372: 
1373:     [:source_branch, :target_branch].each { |attr| validate_branch_name(attr) }
1374:

1824:   def cache_closing_issues_after_merge!(current_user)
1825:     return unless merge_commit || squash_commit
1826:

1828: 
1829:     squash_and_merge_commit_issue_ids = Gitlab::ClosingIssueExtractor.new(project, current_user)
1830:       .closed_by_message(messages.join("\n"))
1831:       .reject { |issue| issue.is_a?(ExternalIssue) }
1832:       .map(&:id)
1833: 
1834:     transaction do
1835:       update_cached_closing_issues_from_description!(squash_and_merge_commit_issue_ids)
1836:       existing_issue_ids = merge_request_closing_issues.pluck(:issue_id)
1837:       issue_ids_to_create = squash_and_merge_commit_issue_ids - existing_issue_ids
1838:

1843:   # If the merge request closes any issues, save this information in the
1844:   # `MergeRequestsClosingIssues` model. This is a performance optimization.
1845:   # Calculating this information for a number of merge requests requires
1846:   # running `ReferenceExtractor` on each of them separately.
1847:   # This optimization does not apply to issues from external sources.
1848:   def cache_merge_request_closes_issues!(current_user = self.author)
1849:     return if closed? || merged?
1850: 
1851:     issues_to_close_ids = closes_issues(current_user).reject { |issue| issue.is_a?(ExternalIssue) }.map(&:id)
1852: 
1853:     transaction do
1854:       merge_request_closing_issues.from_mr_description.delete_all
1855: 
1856:       updated_issue_ids = update_cached_closing_issues_from_description!(issues_to_close_ids)
1857:       issue_ids_to_create = issues_to_close_ids - updated_issue_ids
1858:       next unless issue_ids_to_create.any?
1859: 
1860:       bulk_insert_cached_closing_issues(issue_ids_to_create)
1861:     end
1862:   end

1864:   def visible_closing_issues_for(current_user = self.author)

1866:       visible_issues = if self.target_project.has_external_issue_tracker?
1867:                          closes_issues(current_user)
1868:                        else
1869:                          Ability.issues_readable_by_user(cached_closes_issues, current_user)
1870:                        end
1871: 
1872:       ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(
1873:         records: visible_issues.select { |issue| issue.is_a?(Issue) },
1874:         associations: :project
1875:       ).call
1876:       # Exclude issues that have been cached but their project setting has been disabled, or they belong to a group
1877:       visible_issues.select do |issue|
1878:         !issue.is_a?(Issue) || issue.autoclose_by_merged_closing_merge_request?
1879:       end
1880:     end

1890: 
1891:       Gitlab::ClosingIssueExtractor.new(project, current_user)

1956:     Gitlab::Git::RefPreloader.collect_ref(source_project_id, Gitlab::Git::BRANCH_REF_PREFIX + self.source_branch)
1957:     Gitlab::Git::RefPreloader.collect_ref(target_project_id, Gitlab::Git::BRANCH_REF_PREFIX + self.target_branch)
1958: 
1959:     nil
1960:   end

1974:   def default_merge_commit_message(include_description: false, user: nil)
1975:     if self.target_project.merge_commit_template.present? && !include_description
1976:       return ::Gitlab::MergeRequests::MessageGenerator.new(merge_request: self, current_user: user).merge_commit_message
1977:     end
1978: 
1979:     closes_issues_references = visible_closing_issues_for.map do |issue|
1980:       issue.to_reference(target_project)
1981:     end
1982: 
1983:     message = [
1984:       "Merge branch '#{source_branch}' into '#{target_branch}'",
1985:       title
1986:     ]
1987: 
1988:     if !include_description && closes_issues_references.present?
1989:       message << "Closes #{closes_issues_references.to_sentence}"
1990:     end
1991: 
1992:     message << description if include_description && description.present?
1993:     message << "See merge request #{to_reference(full: true)}"
1994: 
1995:     message.join("\n\n")
1996:   end

2001:     squash_commit_message || title

2403:   # Exists only for merged merge requests
2404:   def commit_to_revert
2405:     return unless merged?
2406: 
2407:     # By default, it's equal to a merge commit
2408:     return merge_commit if merge_commit
2409: 
2410:     # But in case of fast-forward merge merge commits are not created
2411:     # To solve that we can use `squash_commit` if the merge request was squashed
2412:     return squash_commit if squash_commit
2413: 
2414:     # Edge case: one commit in the merge request without merge or squash commit
2415:     return project.commit(diff_head_sha) if commits_count == 1
2416: 
2417:     nil
2418:   end

2420:   def commit_to_cherry_pick

2424:   def can_be_reverted?(current_user)
2425:     return false unless merged_at
2426: 
2427:     commit = commit_to_revert
2428:     return false unless commit
2429: 
2430:     # It is not guaranteed that Note#created_at will be strictly later than
2431:     # MergeRequestMetric#merged_at. Nanoseconds on MySQL may break this
2432:     # comparison, as will a HA environment if clocks are not *precisely*
2433:     # synchronized. Add a minute's leeway to compensate for both possibilities
2434:     cutoff = merged_at - 1.minute
2435: 
2436:     notes_association = notes_with_associations.where('created_at >= ?', cutoff)
2437: 
2438:     !commit.has_been_reverted?(current_user, notes_association)
2439:   end

2441:   def merged_at
2442:     strong_memoize(:merged_at) do
2443:       next unless merged?
2444: 
2445:       metrics&.merged_at ||
2446:         merge_event&.created_at ||
2447:         resource_state_events.find_by(state: :merged)&.created_at ||
2448:         notes.system.reorder(nil).find_by(note: 'merged')&.created_at
2449:     end
2450:   end

2460:   # rubocop: disable CodeReuse/ServiceClass
2461:   def update_diff_discussion_positions(old_diff_refs:, new_diff_refs:, current_user: nil)
2462:     return unless has_complete_diff_refs?
2463:     return if new_diff_refs == old_diff_refs
2464: 
2465:     active_diff_discussions = self.notes.new_diff_notes.discussions.select do |discussion|
2466:       discussion.active?(old_diff_refs)
2467:     end
2468:     return if active_diff_discussions.empty?
2469: 
2470:     paths = active_diff_discussions.flat_map { |n| n.diff_file.paths }.uniq
2471: 
2472:     active_discussions_resolved = active_diff_discussions.all?(&:resolved?)
2473: 
2474:     service = Discussions::UpdateDiffPositionService.new(
2475:       self.project,
2476:       current_user,
2477:       old_diff_refs: old_diff_refs,
2478:       new_diff_refs: new_diff_refs,
2479:       paths: paths
2480:     )
2481: 
2482:     active_diff_discussions.each do |discussion|
2483:       service.execute(discussion)
2484:       discussion.clear_memoized_values
2485:     end
2486: 
2487:     # If they were all already resolved, this method will have already been called.
2488:     # If they all don't get resolved, we don't need to call the method
2489:     # If they go from unresolved -> resolved, then we call the method
2490:     if !active_discussions_resolved &&
2491:         active_diff_discussions.all?(&:resolved?) &&
2492:         project.resolve_outdated_diff_discussions?
2493:       MergeRequests::ResolvedDiscussionNotificationService
2494:         .new(project: project, current_user: current_user)
2495:         .execute(self)
2496:     end
2497:   end

2506:   def enqueue_keep_around_commit

2730:   def diff_head_pipeline_considered_in_progress?
2731:     return true if pipeline_creating?

2733:     pipeline = diff_head_pipeline
2734:     return false unless pipeline
2735: 
2736:     # We allow auto-merge on blocked pipelines when "Pipelines must succeed" is
2737:     # enabled, because in that case the pipeline blocks the merge. When
2738:     # "Pipelines must succeed" is disabled, immediate merges are neither blocked
2739:     # nor discouraged on blocked pipelines, so auto merge should not wait for
2740:     # the pipeline to finish.
2741:     if only_allow_merge_if_pipeline_succeeds?
2742:       !pipeline.complete?
2743:     else
2744:       pipeline.active? || pipeline.created?
2745:     end
2746:   end

2841:   def commit_exists?(sha)
2842:     # We query the SHA from `merge_request_commits_metadata` table first and
2843:     # fallback to querying them from `merge_request_diff_commits` if doesn't match
2844:     # anything. That is to check if commit exists but the records are old and there
2845:     # are no `merge_request_commits_metadata_id` set for them since they're not
2846:     # backfilled yet.
2847:     return true if MergeRequest::CommitsMetadata
2848:       .where(project: project, sha: sha)
2849:       .where_exists(
2850:         MergeRequestDiffCommit
2851:           .where('merge_request_diff_commits.merge_request_commits_metadata_id = merge_request_commits_metadata.id')
2852:           .where_exists(
2853:             merge_request_diffs.where('merge_request_diffs.id = merge_request_diff_commits.merge_request_diff_id')
2854:           )
2855:       )
2856:       .exists?
2857: 
2858:     all_commits.exists?(sha: sha)
2859:   end

2957:   # Returns true when the in-memory state_id no longer matches what the primary
2958:   # database holds. This can happen because the instance was loaded from a
2959:   # replica that lagged behind primary, or because another process transitioned
2960:   # the MR (e.g. lock_mr, mark_as_merged) after this instance was loaded. The
2961:   # caller uses this to skip reload_diff and avoid writing an empty diff on top
2962:   # of a valid merged diff.
2963:   def state_changed_since_load?
2964:     primary_state_id = ::Gitlab::Database::LoadBalancing::SessionMap
2965:       .current(self.class.load_balancer)
2966:       .use_primary { self.class.where(id: id).pick(:state_id) }
2967: 
2968:     primary_state_id != state_id
2969:   end

2976:   def committer_emails_from_diff
2977:     return [] unless merge_request_diff&.persisted?
2978: 
2979:     union = Arel::Nodes::Union.new(
2980:       committer_emails_via_metadata_query,
2981:       committer_emails_via_direct_query
2982:     )
2983: 
2984:     ApplicationRecord.connection.select_values(union.to_sql)
2985:   end
2986:   strong_memoize_attr :committer_emails_from_diff
2987: 
2988:   def committer_emails_via_metadata_query
2989:     dc = MergeRequestDiffCommit.arel_table
2990:     m = MergeRequest::CommitsMetadata.arel_table
2991:     u = MergeRequest::DiffCommitUser.arel_table
2992: 
2993:     dc.project(u[:email])
2994:       .join(m).on(
2995:         m[:id].eq(dc[:merge_request_commits_metadata_id])
2996:         .and(m[:project_id].eq(target_project_id))
2997:       )
2998:       .join(u).on(u[:id].eq(m[:committer_id]))
2999:       .where(dc[:merge_request_diff_id].eq(merge_request_diff.id))
3000:       .where(u[:email].not_eq(nil))
3001:   end
3002: 
3003:   def committer_emails_via_direct_query
3004:     dc = MergeRequestDiffCommit.arel_table
3005:     u = MergeRequest::DiffCommitUser.arel_table
3006: 
3007:     dc.project(u[:email])
3008:       .join(u).on(u[:id].eq(dc[:committer_id]))
3009:       .where(dc[:merge_request_diff_id].eq(merge_request_diff.id))
3010:       .where(dc[:merge_request_commits_metadata_id].eq(nil))
3011:       .where(u[:email].not_eq(nil))
3012:   end

3029:   def bulk_insert_cached_closing_issues(issue_ids_to_create)
3030:     now = Time.zone.now
3031:     new_associations = issue_ids_to_create.map do |issue_id|
3032:       MergeRequestsClosingIssues.new(
3033:         issue_id: issue_id,
3034:         merge_request_id: id,
3035:         from_mr_description: true,
3036:         link_type: :closes,
3037:         created_at: now,
3038:         updated_at: now
3039:       )
3040:     end
3041: 
3042:     # We can't skip validations here in bulk insert as we don't have a unique constraint on the DB.
3043:     # We can skip validations once we have validated the unique constraint
3044:     # TODO: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/issues/456965
3045:     MergeRequestsClosingIssues.bulk_insert!(new_associations, batch_size: 100)
3046:   end

3066:   def with_merge_data_sync(merge_request_block, merge_data_block)
3067:     if dual_write_to_merge_data_ff_enabled?
3068:       # We want to ensure the new merge_data is initialized with the current
3069:       # attributes before we update it.
3070:       ensure_merge_data
3071:       result = merge_request_block.call
3072: 
3073:       begin
3074:         merge_data_block.call
3075: 
3076:       rescue StandardError => e
3077:         Gitlab::ErrorTracking.track_exception(e,
3078:           message: "Failed to sync MergeData",
3079:           merge_request_id: id
3080:         )
3081:       end
3082: 
3083:       result
3084:     else
3085:       merge_request_block.call
3086:     end
3087:   end
3088: 
3089:   def with_single_transaction_merge_data_sync(merge_request_block, merge_data_block)
3090:     if dual_write_to_merge_data_ff_enabled?
3091:       result = nil
3092:       ensure_merge_data
3093: 
3094:       ApplicationRecord.transaction do
3095:         result = merge_request_block.call
3096:         merge_data_block.call
3097:       end
3098: 
3099:       result
3100:     else
3101:       merge_request_block.call
3102:     end
3103:   end
3104: 
3105:   def save_merge_data_changes
3106:     return unless dual_write_to_merge_data_ff_enabled?
3107: 
3108:     ensure_merge_data
3109:     return unless merge_data&.changed?
3110: 
3111:     # id is set when merge_request gets saved so we need to manually
3112:     #   set merge_request_id here if it is not already set.
3113:     merge_data.merge_request_id ||= id
3114:     merge_data.project_id ||= target_project_id
3115:     merge_data.save!
3116:   end
3117: 
3118:   def dual_write_to_merge_data_ff_enabled?

3146:   def with_rebase_lock
3147:     with_retried_nowait_lock { yield }
3148:   end
3149: 
3150:   # If the merge request is idle in transaction or has a SELECT FOR
3151:   # UPDATE, we don't want to block indefinitely or this could cause a
3152:   # queue of SELECT FOR UPDATE calls. Instead, try to get the lock for
3153:   # 5 s before raising an error to the user.
3154:   def with_retried_nowait_lock
3155:     # Try at most 0.25 + (1.5 * .25) + (1.5^2 * .25) ... (1.5^5 * .25) = 5.2 s to get the lock
3156:     Retriable.retriable(on: ActiveRecord::LockWaitTimeout, tries: 6, base_interval: 0.25) do
3157:       with_lock('FOR UPDATE NOWAIT') do
3158:         yield
3159:       end
3160:     end
3161:   rescue ActiveRecord::LockWaitTimeout => e
3162:     Gitlab::ErrorTracking.track_exception(e)
3163:     raise RebaseLockTimeout, _('Failed to enqueue the rebase operation, possibly due to a long-lived transaction. Try again later.')
3164:   end

3: # == Issuable concern
4: #
5: # Contains common functionality shared between Issues and MergeRequests
6: #
7: # Used by Issue, MergeRequest, Epic
8: #
9: module Issuable
10:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
11:   include Gitlab::SQL::Pattern
12:   include Redactable
13:   include CacheMarkdownField
14:   include Participable
15:   include Mentionable
16:   include Milestoneable
17:   include Subscribable
18:   include StripAttribute
19:   include Awardable
20:   include Taskable
21:   include Importable
22:   include Transitionable
23:   include Editable
24:   include AfterCommitQueue
25:   include Sortable
26:   include CreatedAtFilterable
27:   include UpdatedAtFilterable
28:   include ClosedAtFilterable
29:   include VersionedDescription
30:   include SortableTitle
31:   include Exportable
32:   include ReportableChanges
33:   include Import::HasImportSource
34: 
35:   TITLE_LENGTH_MAX = 255
36:   SEARCHABLE_FIELDS = %w[title description].freeze
37:   MAX_NUMBER_OF_ASSIGNEES_OR_REVIEWERS = 200
38: 
39:   STATE_ID_MAP = {
40:     opened: 1,
41:     closed: 2,
42:     merged: 3,
43:     locked: 4
44:   }.with_indifferent_access.freeze
45: 
46:   included do
47:     cache_markdown_field :title, pipeline: :single_line
48:     cache_markdown_field :description, issuable_reference_expansion_enabled: true
49: 
50:     redact_field :description
51: 
52:     belongs_to :author, class_name: 'User'
53:     belongs_to :updated_by, class_name: 'User'
54:     belongs_to :last_edited_by, class_name: 'User'
55: 
56:     has_many :notes, -> { extending Notes::ParticipantAssociationsExtension }, as: :noteable, inverse_of: :noteable, dependent: :destroy # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordDependent
57:     has_many :notes_with_possible_mentions, -> {
58:       Note.from_union(
59:         [
60:           user,
61:           system.with_possible_mentions
62:         ], remove_duplicates: false
63:       ).extending(Notes::ParticipantAssociationsExtension)
64:     }, class_name: 'Note', as: :noteable, inverse_of: :noteable
65: 
66:     has_many :note_authors, -> { distinct }, through: :notes, source: :author
67:     has_many :user_note_authors, -> { distinct.where("notes.system = false") }, through: :notes, source: :author
68: 
69:     has_many :label_links, as: :target, inverse_of: :target
70:     has_many :labels, through: :label_links
71:     has_many :todos, as: :target
72: 
73:     delegate :name,
74:       :email,
75:       :public_email,
76:       to: :author,
77:       allow_nil: true,
78:       prefix: true
79: 
80:     validates :author, presence: true
81:     validates :title, presence: true, length: { maximum: TITLE_LENGTH_MAX }
82:     # we validate the description against the limit only for Issuables being created and on updates if
83:     # the description changes to avoid breaking the existing Issuables which may have their descriptions longer
84:     validates :description, bytesize: { maximum: -> { Gitlab::CurrentSettings.description_and_note_max_size } },
85:       if: :validate_description_length?
86:     validate :validate_assignee_size_length, unless: :importing?
87: 
88:     before_validation :truncate_description_on_import!
89: 
90:     scope :authored, ->(user) { where(author_id: user) }
91:     scope :not_authored, ->(user) { where.not(author_id: user) }
92:     scope :recent, -> { reorder(id: :desc) }
93:     scope :of_projects, ->(ids) { where(project_id: ids) }
94:     scope :with_state, ->(*states) { where(state_id: states.flatten.map { |s| STATE_ID_MAP[s] }) }
95:     scope :opened, -> { with_state(:opened) }
96:     scope :closed, -> { with_state(:closed) }
97: 
98:     # rubocop:disable GitlabSecurity/SqlInjection
99:     # The `assignee_association_name` method is not an user input.
100:     scope :assigned, -> do
101:       where("EXISTS (SELECT TRUE FROM #{assignee_association_name}_assignees \
102:         WHERE #{assignee_association_name}_id = #{assignee_association_name}s.id)")
103:     end
104:     scope :unassigned, -> do
105:       where("NOT EXISTS (SELECT TRUE FROM #{assignee_association_name}_assignees \
106:         WHERE #{assignee_association_name}_id = #{assignee_association_name}s.id)")
107:     end
108:     scope :assigned_to, ->(users) do
109:       assignees_class = reflect_on_association("#{assignee_association_name}_assignees").klass
110: 
111:       condition = assignees_class.where(user_id: users)
112:         .where(Arel.sql("#{assignee_association_name}_id = #{assignee_association_name}s.id"))
113:       where(condition.arel.exists)
114:     end
115:     scope :not_assigned_to, ->(users) do
116:       assignees_class = reflect_on_association("#{assignee_association_name}_assignees").klass
117:

265:     # Searches for records with a matching title.
266:     #
267:     # This method uses ILIKE on PostgreSQL.
268:     #
269:     # query - The search query as a String
270:     #
271:     # Returns an ActiveRecord::Relation.
272:     def search(query)
273:       fuzzy_search(query, [:title])
274:     end

276:     def gfm_autocomplete_search(query)
277:       issuables_cte = Gitlab::SQL::CTE.new(table_name, without_order)
278: 
279:       search_conditions = unscoped.where(
280:         'title ILIKE :pattern',
281:         pattern: "%#{sanitize_sql_like(query)}%"
282:       )
283: 
284:       if query.match?(/\A\d+\z/)
285:         search_conditions = search_conditions.or(
286:           unscoped.where('iid::text LIKE :pattern', pattern: "#{query}%")
287:         )
288:       end
289: 
290:       unscoped
291:         .with(issuables_cte.to_arel)
292:         .from(issuables_cte.table)
293:         .merge(search_conditions)
294:         .order(issuables_cte.table[:id].desc)
295:     end

303:     # Override this on subclasses if different states are needed
304:     #

306:     def available_state_names
307:       [:opened, :closed]
308:     end

310:     # Searches for records with a matching title or description.
311:     #
312:     # This method uses ILIKE on PostgreSQL.
313:     #
314:     # query - The search query as a String
315:     # matched_columns - Modify the scope of the query. 'title', 'description' or joining them with a comma.
316:     #
317:     # Returns an ActiveRecord::Relation.
318:     def full_search(query, matched_columns: nil, use_minimum_char_limit: true)
319:       if matched_columns
320:         matched_columns = matched_columns.to_s.split(',')
321:         matched_columns &= SEARCHABLE_FIELDS
322:         matched_columns.map!(&:to_sym)
323:       end
324: 
325:       search_columns = matched_columns.presence || [:title, :description]
326: 
327:       fuzzy_search(query, search_columns, use_minimum_char_limit: use_minimum_char_limit)
328:     end

334:     def sort_by_attribute(method, excluded_labels: [])
335:       sorted =
336:         case method.to_s
337:         when 'downvotes_desc'
338:           then order_downvotes_desc
339:         when 'label_priority', 'label_priority_asc'
340:           then order_labels_priority(excluded_labels: excluded_labels)
341:         when 'label_priority_desc'
342:           then order_labels_priority('DESC', excluded_labels: excluded_labels)
343:         when 'milestone', 'milestone_due_asc'
344:           then order_milestone_due_asc
345:         when 'milestone_due_desc'
346:           then order_milestone_due_desc
347:         when 'popularity_asc'
348:           then order_upvotes_asc
349:         when 'popularity', 'popularity_desc', 'upvotes_desc'
350:           then order_upvotes_desc
351:         when 'priority', 'priority_asc'
352:           then order_due_date_and_labels_priority(excluded_labels: excluded_labels)
353:         when 'priority_desc'
354:           then order_due_date_and_labels_priority('DESC', excluded_labels: excluded_labels)
355:         when 'title_asc'
356:           then order_title_asc.with_order_id_desc
357:         when 'title_desc'
358:           then order_title_desc.with_order_id_desc
359:         else order_by(method)
360:         end
361: 
362:       # Break ties with the ID column for pagination
363:       sorted.with_order_id_desc
364:     end
365: 
366:     def order_due_date_and_labels_priority(direction = 'ASC', excluded_labels: [])
367:       # The order_ methods also modify the query in other ways:
368:       #
369:       # - For milestones, we add a JOIN.
370:       # - For label priority, we change the SELECT, and add a GROUP BY.#
371:       #
372:       # After doing those, we need to reorder to the order we want. The existing
373:       # ORDER BYs won't work because:
374:       #
375:       # 1. We need milestone due date first.
376:       # 2. We can't ORDER BY a column that isn't in the GROUP BY and doesn't
377:       #    have an aggregate function applied, so we do a useless MIN() instead.
378:       #
379:       milestones_due_date = Milestone.arel_table[:due_date].minimum
380:       milestones_due_date_with_direction = direction == 'ASC' ? milestones_due_date.asc : milestones_due_date.desc
381: 
382:       highest_priority_arel = Arel.sql('highest_priority')
383:       highest_priority_arel_with_direction = direction == 'ASC' ? highest_priority_arel.asc : highest_priority_arel.desc
384: 
385:       order_milestone_due_asc
386:         .order_labels_priority(excluded_labels: excluded_labels, extra_select_columns: [milestones_due_date])
387:         .reorder(milestones_due_date_with_direction.nulls_last, highest_priority_arel_with_direction.nulls_last)
388:     end
389: 
390:     def order_labels_priority(direction = 'ASC', excluded_labels: [], extra_select_columns: [], with_cte: false)
391:       highest_priority = highest_label_priority(
392:         target_type: name == "WorkItem" ? "Issue" : name,
393:         target_column: "#{table_name}.id",
394:         project_column: "#{table_name}.#{project_foreign_key}",
395:         excluded_labels: excluded_labels
396:       ).to_sql
397: 
398:       # When using CTE make sure to select the same columns that are on the group_by clause.
399:       # This prevents errors when ignored columns are present in the database.
400:       issuable_columns = with_cte ? issue_grouping_columns(use_cte: with_cte) : "#{table_name}.*"
401:       group_columns = issue_grouping_columns(use_cte: with_cte) + ["highest_priorities.label_priority"]
402: 
403:       extra_select_columns.unshift("highest_priorities.label_priority as highest_priority")
404: 
405:       highest_priority_arel = Arel.sql('highest_priority')
406:       highest_priority_arel_with_direction = direction == 'ASC' ? highest_priority_arel.asc : highest_priority_arel.desc
407: 
408:       select(issuable_columns)
409:         .select(extra_select_columns)
410:         .from(table_name.to_s)
411:         .joins("JOIN LATERAL(#{highest_priority}) as highest_priorities ON TRUE")
412:         .group(group_columns)
413:         .reorder(highest_priority_arel_with_direction.nulls_last)
414:     end

416:     def with_label(title, sort = nil)
417:       if title.is_a?(Array) && title.size > 1
418:         joins(:labels).where(labels: { title: title }).group(*grouping_columns(sort))
419:           .having("COUNT(DISTINCT labels.title) = #{title.size}")
420:       else
421:         joins(:labels).where(labels: { title: title })
422:       end
423:     end

433:     # Includes table keys in group by clause when sorting
434:     # preventing errors in Postgres
435:     #
436:     # Returns an array of Arel columns
437:     #
438:     def grouping_columns(sort, use_cte: false)
439:       sort = sort.to_s
440: 
441:       # When the relation has been wrapped in a CTE by the search optimisation,
442:       # Postgres cannot infer functional dependency on the CTE's columns even
443:       # though CTE aliases `merge_requests`. Grouping by every issuable column
444:       # satisfies PG without changing query semantics.
445:       grouping_columns = if use_cte
446:                            attribute_names.map { |attr| arel_table[attr.to_sym] }
447:                          else
448:                            [arel_table[:id]]
449:                          end
450: 
451:       if %w[milestone_due_desc milestone_due_asc milestone].include?(sort)
452:         milestone_table = Milestone.arel_table
453:         grouping_columns << milestone_table[:id]
454:         grouping_columns << milestone_table[:due_date]
455:       elsif %w[merged_at_desc merged_at_asc merged_at].include?(sort)
456:         grouping_columns << MergeRequest::Metrics.arel_table[:id]
457:         grouping_columns << MergeRequest::Metrics.arel_table[:merged_at]
458:       elsif %w[closed_at_desc closed_at_asc closed_at].include?(sort)
459:         grouping_columns << MergeRequest::Metrics.arel_table[:id]
460:         grouping_columns << MergeRequest::Metrics.arel_table[:latest_closed_at]
461:       end
462: 
463:       grouping_columns
464:     end
465: 
466:     # Includes all table keys in group by clause when sorting
467:     # preventing errors in Postgres when using CTE search optimization
468:     #
469:     # Returns an array of Arel columns
470:     #
471:     def issue_grouping_columns(use_cte: false)
472:       if use_cte
473:         attribute_names.map { |attr| arel_table[attr.to_sym] }
474:       else
475:         [arel_table[:id]]
476:       end
477:     end

541:   # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity -- Related issue: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/issues/437679
542:   def hook_association_changes(old_associations)
543:     changes = {}
544: 
545:     if old_assignees(old_associations) != assignees
546:       changes[:assignees] = [old_assignees(old_associations).map(&:hook_attrs), assignees.map(&:hook_attrs)]
547:     end
548: 
549:     if old_labels(old_associations) != labels
550:       changes[:labels] = [old_labels(old_associations).map(&:hook_attrs), labels.map(&:hook_attrs)]
551:     end
552: 
553:     if supports_severity? && old_severity(old_associations) != severity
554:       changes[:severity] = [old_severity(old_associations), severity]
555:     end
556: 
557:     if is_a?(MergeRequest) && old_target_branch(old_associations) != target_branch
558:       changes[:target_branch] = [old_target_branch(old_associations), target_branch]
559:     end
560: 
561:     if supports_escalation? && escalation_status &&
562:         old_escalation_status(old_associations) != escalation_status.status_name
563:       changes[:escalation_status] = [old_escalation_status(old_associations), escalation_status.status_name]
564:     end
565: 
566:     if respond_to?(:total_time_spent) && old_total_time_spent(old_associations) != total_time_spent
567:       changes[:total_time_spent] = [old_total_time_spent(old_associations), total_time_spent]
568:       changes[:time_change] = [old_time_change(old_associations), time_change]
569:     end
570: 
571:     changes
572:   end

575:   def reviewers_hook_attrs(re_requested_reviewer_id: nil)
576:     return [] unless allows_reviewers?
577: 
578:     merge_request_reviewers.includes(:reviewer).map do |mr_reviewer|
579:       attrs = mr_reviewer.reviewer.hook_attrs.merge(state: mr_reviewer.state)
580:       attrs[:re_requested] = !!(re_requested_reviewer_id && mr_reviewer.reviewer.id == re_requested_reviewer_id)
581:       attrs
582:     end
583:   end
584: 
585:   def hook_reviewer_changes(old_associations)
586:     changes = {}
587: 
588:     re_requested_reviewer_id = old_associations.fetch(:re_requested_reviewer_id, nil)
589:     old_reviewer_attrs = old_associations.fetch(:reviewers_hook_attrs, [])
590:     current_reviewer_attrs = reviewers_hook_attrs(re_requested_reviewer_id: re_requested_reviewer_id)
591: 
592:     changes[:reviewers] = [old_reviewer_attrs, current_reviewer_attrs] if old_reviewer_attrs != current_reviewer_attrs
593: 
594:     changes
595:   end

602:       changes.merge!(hook_reviewer_changes(old_associations)) if allows_reviewers?
603:     end

606:       user: user,

608:       action: action,
609:       actioned_at: actioned_at)

662:   def notes_with_associations(relation = notes)
663:     # If A has_many Bs, and B has_many Cs, and you do
664:     # `A.includes(b: :c).each { |a| a.b.includes(:c) }`, sadly ActiveRecord
665:     # will do the inclusion again. So, we check if all notes in the relation
666:     # already have their authors loaded (possibly because the scope
667:     # `inc_notes_with_associations` was used) and skip the inclusion if that's
668:     # the case.
669:     includes = []
670:     includes << :author unless relation.authors_loaded?
671:     includes << :award_emoji unless relation.award_emojis_loaded?
672: 
673:     if persisted? && includes.any?
674:       relation.includes(includes)
675:     else
676:       relation
677:     end
678:   end

3: module Noteable
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   # This object is used to gather noteable meta data for list displays
7:   # avoiding n+1 queries and improving performance.
8:   NoteableMeta = Struct.new(:user_notes_count)
9: 
10:   MAX_NOTES_LIMIT = 5_000

12:   class_methods do
13:     # `Noteable` class names that support replying to individual notes.
14:     def replyable_types
15:       %w[Issue MergeRequest AbuseReport WikiPage::Meta]
16:     end
17: 
18:     # `Noteable` class names that support resolvable notes.
19:     def resolvable_types
20:       %w[Issue MergeRequest DesignManagement::Design AbuseReport WikiPage::Meta]
21:     end
22: 
23:     # `Noteable` class names that support creating/forwarding individual notes.

26:     end

29:   # The timestamp of the note (e.g. the :created_at or :updated_at attribute if provided via
30:   # API call)

32:     @system_note_timestamp || Time.current # rubocop:disable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
33:   end
34: 
35:   attr_writer :system_note_timestamp
36: 
37:   def base_class_name
38:     self.class.base_class.name
39:   end
40: 
41:   # Convert this Noteable class name to a format usable by notifications.
42:   #
43:   # Examples:
44:   #
45:   #   noteable.class           # => MergeRequest
46:   #   noteable.human_class_name # => "merge request"
47:   def human_class_name
48:     @human_class_name ||= base_class_name.titleize.downcase
49:   end
50: 
51:   def supports_resolvable_notes?
52:     self.class.resolvable_types.include?(base_class_name)
53:   end
54: 
55:   def supports_discussions?
56:     DiscussionNote.noteable_types.include?(base_class_name)
57:   end
58: 
59:   def supports_replying_to_individual_notes?
60:     supports_discussions? && self.class.replyable_types.include?(base_class_name)
61:   end
62: 
63:   def supports_replying_to_system_notes?
64:     false
65:   end
66: 
67:   def supports_creating_notes_by_email?
68:     self.class.email_creatable_types.include?(base_class_name)
69:   end
70: 
71:   def supports_suggestion?
72:     false
73:   end
74: 
75:   def discussions_rendered_on_frontend?
76:     false
77:   end
78: 
79:   def preloads_discussion_diff_highlighting?
80:     false
81:   end
82: 
83:   def has_any_diff_note_positions?
84:     notes.any? && DiffNotePosition.where(note: notes).exists?
85:   end
86: 
87:   def discussion_notes
88:     notes
89:   end
90: 
91:   delegate :find_discussion, to: :discussion_notes
92: 
93:   def discussions
94:     @discussions ||= discussion_notes
95:       .inc_relations_for_view(self)
96:       .discussions(self)
97:   end
98: 
99:   def discussion_ids_relation
100:     notes.select(:discussion_id)
101:       .group(:discussion_id)
102:       .order('MIN(created_at), MIN(id)')
103:   end
104: 
105:   # This does not consider OutOfContextDiscussions in MRs
106:   # where notes from commits are overridden so that they have
107:   # the same discussion_id
108:   def discussion_root_note_ids(notes_filter:, sort: :created_asc)
109:     sort = :created_asc if sort.nil?
110:     relations = []
111: 
112:     relations << discussion_notes.select(
113:       "'notes' AS table_name",
114:       'MIN(id) AS id',
115:       'MIN(created_at) AS created_at',
116:       'ARRAY_AGG(id) AS ids'
117:     ).with_notes_filter(notes_filter)
118:      .group(:discussion_id)
119: 
120:     if notes_filter != UserPreference::NOTES_FILTERS[:only_comments]
121:       relations += synthetic_note_ids_relations
122:     end
123: 
124:     discussions = Note.from_union(relations, remove_duplicates: false)
125:                     .select(:table_name, :id, :created_at, :ids)
126: 
127:     case sort
128:     when :created_asc
129:       discussions.order_created_at_id_asc
130:     when :created_desc
131:       discussions.order_created_at_id_desc
132:     else
133:       raise ArgumentError, 'Invalid sort order'
134:     end
135:   end
136: 
137:   def capped_notes_count(max)
138:     notes.limit(max).count
139:   end
140: 
141:   def grouped_diff_discussions(...)
142:     # Doesn't use `discussion_notes`, because this may include commit diff notes
143:     # besides MR diff notes, that we do not want to display on the MR Changes tab.
144:     notes.inc_relations_for_view(self).grouped_diff_discussions(...)
145:   end
146: 
147:   # rubocop:disable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
148:   def resolvable_discussions
149:     @resolvable_discussions ||=
150:       if defined?(@discussions)
151:         @discussions.select(&:resolvable?)
152:       else
153:         discussion_notes.resolvable.discussions(self)
154:       end
155:   end
156:   # rubocop:enable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
157: 
158:   def discussions_resolvable?
159:     resolvable_discussions.any?(&:resolvable?)
160:   end
161: 
162:   def discussions_resolved?
163:     discussions_resolvable? && resolvable_discussions.none?(&:to_be_resolved?)
164:   end
165: 
166:   def discussions_to_be_resolved
167:     @discussions_to_be_resolved ||= resolvable_discussions.select(&:to_be_resolved?)
168:   end
169: 
170:   def discussions_can_be_resolved_by?(user)
171:     discussions_to_be_resolved.all? { |discussion| discussion.can_resolve?(user) }
172:   end
173: 
174:   def lockable?
175:     [MergeRequest, Issue].include?(self.class)
176:   end
177: 
178:   def real_time_notes_enabled?
179:     false
180:   end
181: 
182:   def broadcast_notes_changed
183:     return unless discussions_rendered_on_frontend?
184:     return unless real_time_notes_enabled?
185: 
186:     Noteable::NotesChannel.broadcast_to(self, event: 'updated')
187:   end
188: 
189:   def after_note_created(_note)
190:     # no-op
191:   end
192: 
193:   def after_note_destroyed(_note)
194:     # no-op
195:   end
196: 
197:   # Email address that an authorized user can send/forward an email to be added directly
198:   # to an issue or merge request.
199:   # example: incoming+h5bp-html5-boilerplate-8-1234567890abcdef123456789-issue-34@localhost.com
200:   def creatable_note_email_address(author)
201:     return unless supports_creating_notes_by_email?
202: 
203:     project_email = project&.new_issuable_address(author, base_class_name.underscore)
204:     return unless project_email
205: 
206:     project_email.sub('@', "-#{iid}@")
207:   end
208: 
209:   def noteable_target_type_name
210:     model_name.singular
211:   end
212: 
213:   def commenters(user: nil)
214:     eligable_notes = notes.user
215: 
216:     eligable_notes = eligable_notes.not_internal unless user&.can?(:read_internal_note, self)
217: 
218:     User.where(id: eligable_notes.select(:author_id).distinct)
219:   end
220: 
221:   private
222: 
223:   # Synthetic system notes don't have discussion IDs because these are generated dynamically
224:   # in Ruby. These are always root notes anyway so we don't need to group by discussion ID.
225:   def synthetic_note_ids_relations
226:     relations = []
227: 
228:     # currently multiple models include Noteable concern, but not all of them support
229:     # all resource events, so we check if given model supports given resource event.
230:     if respond_to?(:resource_label_events)
231:       relations << resource_label_events.select("'resource_label_events'", 'MIN(id)', :created_at, 'ARRAY_AGG(id)')
232:                      .group(:created_at, :user_id)
233:     end
234: 
235:     if respond_to?(:resource_state_events)
236:       relations << resource_state_events.select("'resource_state_events'", :id, :created_at, 'ARRAY_FILL(id, ARRAY[1])')
237:     end
238: 
239:     if respond_to?(:resource_milestone_events)
240:       relations << resource_milestone_events.select("'resource_milestone_events'", :id, :created_at, 'ARRAY_FILL(id, ARRAY[1])')
241:     end
242: 
243:     relations
244:   end
245: end
246: 
247: Noteable.extend(Noteable::ClassMethods)
248: 
249: Noteable::ClassMethods.prepend_mod_with('Noteable::ClassMethods')
250: Noteable.prepend_mod_with('Noteable')

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # == Referable concern
4: #
5: # Contains functionality related to making a model referable in Markdown, such
6: # as "#1", "!2", "~3", etc.
7: module Referable
8:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
9: 
10:   # Returns the String necessary to reference this object in Markdown
11:   #
12:   # from - Referring parent object
13:   #
14:   # This should be overridden by the including class.
15:   #
16:   # Examples:
17:   #
18:   #   Issue.first.to_reference               # => "#1"
19:   #   Issue.last.to_reference(other_project) # => "cross-project#1"
20:   #
21:   # Returns a String
22:   def to_reference(_from = nil, full:)
23:     ''
24:   end
25: 
26:   # If this referable object can serve as the base for the
27:   # reference of child objects (e.g. projects are the base of
28:   # issues), but it is formatted differently, then you may wish
29:   # to override this method.
30:   def to_reference_base(from = nil, full:, absolute_path: false)
31:     to_reference(from, full: full)
32:   end
33: 
34:   def reference_link_text(from = nil)
35:     to_reference(from)
36:   end
37: 
38:   included do
39:     alias_method :non_referable_inspect, :inspect
40:     alias_method :inspect, :referable_inspect
41:   end
42: 
43:   def referable_inspect
44:     if respond_to?(:id)
45:       "#<#{self.class.name} id:#{id} #{to_reference(full: true)}>"
46:     else
47:       "#<#{self.class.name} #{to_reference(full: true)}>"
48:     end
49:   end
50: 
51:   class_methods do
52:     # The character that prefixes the actual reference identifier
53:     #
54:     # This should be overridden by the including class.
55:     #
56:     # Examples:
57:     #
58:     #   Issue.reference_prefix        # => '#'
59:     #   MergeRequest.reference_prefix # => '!'
60:     #
61:     # Returns a String
62:     def reference_prefix
63:       ''
64:     end
65: 
66:     # Regexp pattern used to match references to this object
67:     #
68:     # This must be overridden by the including class.
69:     #
70:     # Returns a Regexp
71:     def reference_pattern
72:       raise NotImplementedError, "#{self} does not implement #{__method__}"
73:     end
74: 
75:     def reference_valid?(reference)
76:       true
77:     end
78: 
79:     def link_reference_pattern
80:       raise NotImplementedError, "#{self} does not implement #{__method__}"
81:     end
82: 
83:     # Regex to match a fully qualified URL to a particular route and pattern
84:     # under a group or project context.
85:     #
86:     # Example use:  WikiPage.link_reference_pattern may be defined as follows:
87:     #
88:     #   def self.link_reference_pattern
89:     #     @link_reference_pattern ||= project_or_group_link_reference_pattern(
90:     #       'wikis',
91:     #       %r{(?<wiki_page>[\/\w-]+)}
92:     #     )
93:     #   end
94:     #
95:     # This produces a regex which matches URLs like:
96:     #
97:     # 'http://gdk.test:3000/flightjs/Flight/-/wikis/henlo-worl/yippee' (wiki_page: '/henlo-worl/yippee')
98:     # 'http://gdk.test:3000/groups/flightjs/-/wikis/home' (wiki_page: 'home')
99:     #
100:     # The regex also matches arbitrary trailing path components if the supplied
101:     # `pattern` does not consume everything, as well as query string and anchor
102:     # (fragment) components. See `link_reference_pattern_suffix` for details.
103:     def project_or_group_link_reference_pattern(route, pattern)
104:       %r{
105:         (?<url>
106:           #{Regexp.escape(Gitlab.config.gitlab.url)}
107:           \/(groups\/)?#{namespace_reference_pattern}
108:           (?:\/\-)?
109:           #{link_reference_pattern_suffix(route, pattern)}
110:         )
111:       }x
112:     end
113: 
114:     # Same as `project_or_group_link_reference_pattern`, except there's no
115:     # provision for 'groups/' and `Project.reference_pattern` is used instead
116:     # of `namespace_reference_pattern`; i.e. a fully-qualified project must
117:     # be specified.
118:     def compose_link_reference_pattern(route, pattern)
119:       %r{
120:         (?<url>
121:           #{Regexp.escape(Gitlab.config.gitlab.url)}
122:           \/#{Project.reference_pattern}
123:           (?:\/\-)?
124:           #{link_reference_pattern_suffix(route, pattern)}
125:         )
126:       }x
127:     end
128: 
129:     # Same as `project_or_group_link_reference_pattern`, except there's no
130:     # provision for a group or pattern at all before the '/-', which is
131:     # non-optional.
132:     #
133:     # Example use:  PersonalSnippet.link_reference_pattern may be defined as follows:
134:     #
135:     #   def self.link_reference_pattern
136:     #     @link_reference_pattern ||=
137:     #       compose_top_level_link_reference_pattern('snippets', /(?<snippet>\d+)/)
138:     #   end
139:     #
140:     # This produces a regex which matches URLs like:
141:     #
142:     # 'http://gdk.test:3000/-/snippets/2468'  (snippet: '2468')
143:     # 'http://gdk-oc.local:13000/-/snippets/2468/raw/main/socks'  (snippet: '2468', path: '/raw/main/socks')
144:     def compose_top_level_link_reference_pattern(route, pattern)
145:       %r{
146:         (?<url>
147:           #{Regexp.escape(Gitlab.config.gitlab.url)}
148:           \/\-
149:           #{link_reference_pattern_suffix(route, pattern)}
150:         )
151:       }x
152:     end
153: 
154:     private
155: 
156:     def link_reference_pattern_suffix(route, pattern)
157:       %r{
158:         \/#{route.is_a?(Regexp) ? route : Regexp.escape(route)}
159:         \/#{pattern}
160:         (?<path>
161:           (\/[a-z0-9_=-]+)*\/*
162:         )?
163:         (?<query>
164:           \?[a-z0-9_=-]+
165:           (&[a-z0-9_=-]+)*
166:         )?
167:         (?<anchor>\#[a-z0-9_-]+)?
168:       }x
169:     end
170:   end
171: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # == TimeTrackable concern
4: #
5: # Contains functionality related to objects that support time tracking.
6: #
7: # Used by Issue and MergeRequest.
8: #
9: 
10: module TimeTrackable
11:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
12: 
13:   include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
14: 
15:   included do
16:     attr_reader :time_spent, :time_spent_user, :spent_at, :summary
17: 
18:     alias_method :time_spent?, :time_spent
19: 
20:     validate :check_time_estimate
21:     validate :check_negative_time_spent
22: 
23:     has_many :timelogs, dependent: :destroy, autosave: true # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordDependent
24:     before_save :set_time_estimate_default_value
25:     after_save :clear_memoized_total_time_spent
26:   end
27: 
28:   def clear_memoized_total_time_spent
29:     clear_memoization(:total_time_spent)
30:   end
31: 
32:   def reset
33:     clear_memoized_total_time_spent
34: 
35:     super
36:   end
37: 
38:   def reload(*args)
39:     clear_memoized_total_time_spent
40: 
41:     super(*args)
42:   end
43: 
44:   # rubocop:disable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
45:   def spend_time(options)
46:     @time_spent = options[:duration]
47:     @time_spent_note_id = options[:note_id]
48:     @time_spent_user = User.find(options[:user_id])
49:     @spent_at = options[:spent_at]
50:     @summary = options[:summary]
51:     @original_total_time_spent = nil
52:     @category_id = category_id(options[:category])
53: 
54:     return if @time_spent == 0
55: 
56:     @timelog = if @time_spent == :reset
57:                  reset_spent_time
58:                else
59:                  add_or_subtract_spent_time
60:                end
61:   end
62:   alias_method :spend_time=, :spend_time
63:   # rubocop:enable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
64: 
65:   def total_time_spent
66:     sum = timelogs.sum(:time_spent)
67: 
68:     # A new restriction has been introduced to limit total time spent to -
69:     # Timelog::MAX_TOTAL_TIME_SPENT
70:     # Since there could be existing records that breach the limit, check and return the maximum/minimum allowed value.
71:     # (some issuable might have total time spent that's negative because a validation was missing.)
72:     sum.clamp(-Timelog::MAX_TOTAL_TIME_SPENT, Timelog::MAX_TOTAL_TIME_SPENT)
73:   end
74:   strong_memoize_attr :total_time_spent
75: 
76:   def human_total_time_spent
77:     Gitlab::TimeTrackingFormatter.output(total_time_spent)
78:   end
79: 
80:   def time_change
81:     @timelog&.time_spent.to_i # rubocop:disable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
82:   end
83: 
84:   def human_time_change
85:     Gitlab::TimeTrackingFormatter.output(time_change)
86:   end
87: 
88:   def human_time_estimate
89:     Gitlab::TimeTrackingFormatter.output(time_estimate)
90:   end
91: 
92:   def time_estimate=(val)
93:     val.is_a?(Integer) ? super([val, Gitlab::Database::MAX_INT_VALUE].min) : super(val)
94:   end
95: 
96:   def time_estimate
97:     super || self.class.column_defaults['time_estimate']
98:   end
99: 
100:   def set_time_estimate_default_value
101:     return if new_record?
102:     return unless has_attribute?(:time_estimate)
103:     # time estimate can be set to nil, in case of an invalid value, e.g. a String instead of a number, in which case
104:     # we should not be overwriting it to default value, but rather have the validation catch the error
105:     return if time_estimate_changed?
106: 
107:     self.time_estimate = self.class.column_defaults['time_estimate'] if read_attribute(:time_estimate).nil?
108:   end
109: 
110:   private
111: 
112:   def reset_spent_time
113:     timelogs.new(time_spent: total_time_spent * -1, user: @time_spent_user) # rubocop:disable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
114:   end
115: 
116:   # rubocop:disable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
117:   def add_or_subtract_spent_time
118:     timelogs.new(
119:       time_spent: time_spent,
120:       note_id: @time_spent_note_id,
121:       user: @time_spent_user,
122:       spent_at: @spent_at,
123:       summary: @summary,
124:       timelog_category_id: @category_id
125:     )
126:   end
127:   # rubocop:enable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
128: 
129:   def check_negative_time_spent
130:     return if time_spent.nil? || time_spent == :reset
131: 
132:     if time_spent < 0 && (time_spent.abs > original_total_time_spent)
133:       errors.add(:base, _('Time to subtract exceeds the total time spent'))
134:     end
135:   end
136: 
137:   # we need to cache the total time spent so multiple calls to #valid?
138:   # doesn't give a false error
139:   def original_total_time_spent
140:     @original_total_time_spent ||= total_time_spent
141:   end
142: 
143:   def check_time_estimate
144:     # we'll set the time_tracking to zero at DB level through default value
145:     return unless time_estimate_changed?
146:     return if read_attribute(:time_estimate).is_a?(Numeric) && read_attribute(:time_estimate) >= 0
147: 
148:     errors.add(:time_estimate, _('must have a valid format and be greater than or equal to zero.'))
149:   end
150: 
151:   def category_id(category)
152:     TimeTracking::TimelogCategory.find_by_name(project&.root_namespace, category).first&.id
153:   end
154: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spammable
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5:   include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
6: 
7:   class_methods do
8:     def attr_spammable(attr, options = {})
9:       spammable_attrs << [attr.to_s, options]
10:     end
11:   end
12: 
13:   included do
14:     has_one :user_agent_detail, as: :subject, dependent: :destroy # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordDependent
15: 
16:     attr_writer :spam
17:     attr_accessor :needs_recaptcha
18:     attr_accessor :spam_log
19: 
20:     alias_method :spam?, :spam
21:     alias_method :needs_recaptcha?, :needs_recaptcha
22: 
23:     # if spam errors are added before validation, they will be wiped
24:     after_validation :invalidate_if_spam, on: [:create, :update]
25: 
26:     cattr_accessor :spammable_attrs, instance_accessor: false do
27:       []
28:     end
29: 
30:     delegate :ip_address, :user_agent, to: :user_agent_detail, allow_nil: true
31:   end
32: 
33:   def spam
34:     !!@spam # rubocop:disable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
35:   end
36: 
37:   def submittable_as_spam_by?(current_user)
38:     current_user && current_user.admin? && submittable_as_spam?
39:   end
40: 
41:   def submittable_as_spam?
42:     if user_agent_detail
43:       user_agent_detail.submittable? && Gitlab::CurrentSettings.current_application_settings.akismet_enabled
44:     else
45:       false
46:     end
47:   end
48: 
49:   def needs_recaptcha!
50:     if self.supports_recaptcha?
51:       self.needs_recaptcha = true
52:     else
53:       self.spam!
54:     end
55:   end
56: 
57:   # Override in Spammable if recaptcha is supported
58:   def supports_recaptcha?
59:     false
60:   end
61: 
62:   ##
63:   # Indicates if a recaptcha should be rendered before allowing this model to be saved.
64:   #
65:   def render_recaptcha?
66:     return false unless Gitlab::Recaptcha.enabled? && supports_recaptcha?
67: 
68:     return false if self.errors.count > 1 # captcha should not be rendered if are still other errors
69: 
70:     self.needs_recaptcha?
71:   end
72: 
73:   def spam!
74:     self.spam = true
75:   end
76: 
77:   def clear_spam_flags!
78:     self.spam = false
79:     self.needs_recaptcha = false
80:   end
81: 
82:   def invalidate_if_spam
83:     if needs_recaptcha? && Gitlab::Recaptcha.enabled? && supports_recaptcha?
84:       recaptcha_error!
85:     elsif needs_recaptcha? || spam?
86:       unrecoverable_spam_error!
87:     end
88:   end
89: 
90:   def recaptcha_error!
91:     self.errors.add(:base, format(_("Your %{spammable_entity_type} has been recognized as spam. "\
92:                     "Please, change the content or solve the reCAPTCHA to proceed."),
93:       spammable_entity_type: spammable_entity_type))
94:   end
95: 
96:   def unrecoverable_spam_error!
97:     self.errors.add(:base, format(_("Your %{spammable_entity_type} has been recognized as spam. "\
98:                     "Please, change the content to proceed."), spammable_entity_type: spammable_entity_type))
99:   end
100: 
101:   def spammable_entity_type
102:     case self
103:     when Issue
104:       _('issue')
105:     when MergeRequest
106:       _('merge request')
107:     when Note
108:       _('comment')
109:     when Snippet
110:       _('snippet')
111:     else
112:       self.class.model_name.human.downcase
113:     end
114:   end
115: 
116:   def spam_title
117:     attr = self.class.spammable_attrs.find do |_, options|
118:       options.fetch(:spam_title, false)
119:     end
120: 
121:     public_send(attr.first) if attr && respond_to?(attr.first.to_sym) # rubocop:disable GitlabSecurity/PublicSend
122:   end
123: 
124:   def spam_description
125:     attr = self.class.spammable_attrs.find do |_, options|
126:       options.fetch(:spam_description, false)
127:     end
128: 
129:     public_send(attr.first) if attr && respond_to?(attr.first.to_sym) # rubocop:disable GitlabSecurity/PublicSend
130:   end
131: 
132:   def spammable_text
133:     result = self.class.spammable_attrs.map do |attr|
134:       public_send(attr.first) # rubocop:disable GitlabSecurity/PublicSend
135:     end
136: 
137:     result.reject(&:blank?).join("\n")
138:   end
139: 
140:   # Override in included class if further checks are necessary
141:   def check_for_spam?(*)
142:     spammable_attribute_changed?
143:   end
144: 
145:   def spammable_attribute_changed?
146:     (changed & self.class.spammable_attrs.to_h.keys).any?
147:   end
148: 
149:   def check_for_spam(user:, action:, extra_features: {})
150:     strong_memoize_with(:check_for_spam, user, action, extra_features) do
151:       Spam::SpamActionService.new(spammable: self, user: user, action: action, extra_features: extra_features).execute
152:     end
153:   end
154: 
155:   # Override in included class if you want to allow possible spam under specific circumstances
156:   def allow_possible_spam?(*)
157:     Gitlab::CurrentSettings.allow_possible_spam
158:   end
159: end
